markdown:
## Original Transformer vs. BERT

Below is a side‑by‑side comparison along the three axes you requested — *architecture role*, *directionality*, and *training objective*.

| Axis | Original Transformer (Vaswani et al., 2017) | BERT (Devlin et al., 2018) |
|---|---|---|
| **Architecture role** | Full **encoder–decoder** network for **sequence transduction** (introduced and evaluated on machine translation, e.g., WMT 2014 EN–DE / EN–FR). "The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or convolutional neural networks that include an encoder and a decoder… We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms." | **Encoder‑only** stack reused from Vaswani et al.: "BERT's model architecture is a multi-layer bidirectional Transformer encoder based on the original implementation described in Vaswani et al. (2017) and released in the tensor2tensor library." Used as a representation/pretraining backbone, not as a transduction system. |
| **Directionality (attention masking)** | **Bidirectional inside the encoder** (no masking) **+ autoregressive / unidirectional inside the decoder.** The decoder explicitly restricts self-attention to past positions: "We also modify the self-attention sub-layer in the decoder stack to prevent positions from attending to subsequent positions. This masking, combined with fact that the output embeddings are offset by one position, ensures that the predictions for position i can depend only on the known outputs at positions less than i." | **Deeply bidirectional in every layer.** "BERT is designed to pretrain deep bidirectional representations from unlabeled text by jointly conditioning on both left and right context in all layers." "BERT alleviates the previously mentioned unidirectionality constraint by using a 'masked language model' (MLM) pre-training objective." |
| **Primary training objective** | **Supervised sequence‑to‑sequence translation:** "the encoder maps an input sequence of symbol representations $(x_1,\dots,x_n)$ to a sequence of continuous representations $\mathbf{z}$; given $\mathbf{z}$, the decoder then generates an output sequence $(y_1,\dots,y_m)$ of symbols one element at a time. At each step the model is auto-regressive." Optimized end‑to‑end on parallel translation corpora. | **Self‑supervised pretraining on unlabeled text** with two tasks: (1) **Masked Language Model (MLM):** "The training data generator chooses 15% of the token positions at random for prediction. If the i-th token is chosen, we replace the i-th token with (1) the [MASK] token 80% of the time (2) a random token 10% of the time (3) the unchanged i-th token 10% of the time." (2) **Next Sentence Prediction (NSP):** "50% of the time B is the actual next sentence that follows A (labeled as IsNext), and 50% of the time it is a random sentence from the corpus (labeled as NotNext)." The pretrained model is then fine‑tuned with one added output layer on downstream tasks. |

### Evidence ids used

- Original Transformer: [1] (abstract), [2] (§3 encoder–decoder / auto‑regressive), [3] (encoder stack), [4] (decoder masking = unidirectional).
- BERT: [5] (abstract — encoder‑only, bidirectional), [6] (§3 — architecture is the Vaswani et al. encoder), [7] (MLM motivation), [8] (contrast with unidirectional LMs), [9] (why standard CLMs can't be bidirectional), [10] (MLM masking proportions), [11] (NSP).

### One‑sentence summary
The original Transformer is a full encoder–decoder, attention‑only sequence‑transduction model in which the encoder sees both directions and the decoder is strictly left‑to‑right; BERT discards the decoder, keeps only the (bidirectional) Transformer encoder, and replaces the translation objective with two self‑supervised pretraining tasks — Masked Language Modeling and Next Sentence Prediction — on unlabeled text.

Sources
[1] Attention Is All You Need, Abstract, p. 1
[2] Attention Is All You Need, 3 Model Architecture, p. 2
[3] Attention Is All You Need, 3.1 Encoder and Decoder Stacks, p. 3
[4] Attention Is All You Need, 3.1 Encoder and Decoder Stacks, p. 3
[5] BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding, Abstract, p. 1
[6] BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding, 3 BERT, p. 3
[7] BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding, 1 Introduction, p. 1
[8] BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding, 1 Introduction, p. 2
[9] BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding, 3.1 Pre-training BERT, p. 4
[10] BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding, 3.1 Pre-training BERT, p. 4
[11] BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding, 3.1 Pre-training BERT, p. 4
